extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AVERMAET Greg attacked at Carrefour de l'Arbre with 15km remaining, formed five-man sprint group, and outkicked STUYVEN Jasper at the velodrome.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET Greg recovered from Wallers crash at 84km to go, proving resilience critical for cobbled Monument success.
STUYVEN Jasper launched velodrome sprint but faded, showing early initiation from distance disadvantageous on Roubaix track.
BOONEN Tom outside top-10 in final edition reveals age degradation hits cobbled specialists harder than other Monument profiles.
SAGAN Peter present in 84km breakaway but absent from decisive 15km move, suggesting inconsistent pacing over extended cobbled racing.

rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Won after mid-race crash recovery, demonstrating mental and physical resilience unmatched in 2017 cobbled season
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: Second place after being caught late suggests strong positioning but lacks finishing power against top sprinters
rider_signals.BOONEN Tom: negative: 13th in final Roubaix confirms decline; four-time champion no longer competitive in Monument finales
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Faded between 84km and 15km decisive moves, revealing stamina limitations on prolonged cobbled efforts
